text: {"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"2","subject":"Spawn Celes: ESL-legal team design","description":"PO has locked 5 axes. Celes spawns with brief + 5 locked + open-for-her items. First check-in expected: 2-3 architecture options + naming proposal. PO confirms shape+naming before Celes drafts full package.","assignedBy":"celes","timestamp":"2026-05-13T07:53:18.966Z"}

[2026-05-13 11:06]

PO ratified all four — with one major correction. Re-fold before drafting.

## Ratifications

1. **Roman names** ✓ (Papinianus / Paulus / Ulpianus / Modestinus / Gaius + Cicero)
2. **Option B** ✓ (6-char, EE/EU asymmetric workload)
3. **MUST-address `[ADVERSARY-FLAG]`** ✓ (your preferred mechanism; Editor MUST address each before T-2 either by folding or by documenting why claim survives)
4. **Repo**: `mitselek/esl-legal` **created (private)**: https://github.com/mitselek/esl-legal

## CORRECTION — team persistence model

PO's exact framing: *"I see this team as long-living support unit and Peterson is just the case nr.1."*

You and I both read "10-day clock + dissolves on memo delivery" from Tobi's brief. Source of the misread (own it cleanly): brief §1+§3+§9 scope the deliverable AROUND the Peterson case; my first-pass work-type sketch (TL + 4 specialists + adversary "for Q1-Q7 scan + memo") inherited that and biased your archetype call. PO has corrected: this is a persistent long-lived per-domain research support unit; Peterson is the first case.

## Design implications to fold in

- **Archetype**: persistent long-lived per-domain research support unit. Activates on new cases, quiet between. Distinct from esl-suvekool (operational, event-driven) and FR/apex-research (methodology). Roman-jurist names fit *better* here — these were career jurists, not one-trial advocates.
- **Cadence**: dormant between cases; low-volume during case windows (~1-3x/week active). Match esl-suvekool pattern.
- **Mission framing in common-prompt**: "ESL legal-affairs research support" — NOT "Peterson defensive scan." Peterson is the worked example; the team's identity is the support function.
- **Repo structure** must support multi-case from day 1. Suggested:
  - `cases/2026-05-peterson/` (this case's research + memo + adversary flags + verbatim quotes)
  - `wiki/statutes/` (frequently-cited EE statutes — AutÕS §13/§17/§18, MTÜS, VÕS, TsMS — accumulated across cases)
  - `wiki/cjeu/` (CJEU case-cards built up over time)
  - `wiki/opposing-parties/` (templates of arguments seen — Peterson's "tagasipöörata ei saa" framing, Kaie's "EL reegel ei käi" categorical, etc.)
  - `wiki/precedents/` (EE case law encountered, with citations)
  - `wiki/jurists/` (EE-licensed jurists worth contacting, with notes)
  - `wiki/index.md` (Cal-style catalog)
- **Gaius's role expands**: not just Peterson-corpus librarian, but cross-case wiki curator. Probably the most load-bearing role on the team's *long-term* value (case-by-case research compounds in his surface). Worth elevating in his prompt — he's not a service role, he's the team's institutional memory.
- **Succession-readiness** like S23: every artifact designed for the *next on-call legal lead*, not for PO specifically.
- **Shutdown protocol**: per-session (FR-style S5 `TeamDelete` on graceful exit). NOT team dissolution. Persistent roster carries between sessions.
- **Restart-readiness on a multi-day clock**: assume sessions may span Peterson (10 days), gap, then case #2 weeks later. Bootstrap hook in `.claude/startup.md` at repo root (your S23 pattern) makes this work.

## Cal-queue revision

- **STRIKE**: "one-shot dissolution team" archetype observation (was based on the misread above)
- **NEW candidate**: "Long-lived per-domain research support unit" archetype, n=1 watch. Distinct from operational-team-archetype (esl-suvekool) and methodology-research (FR/apex-research). Promotion trigger: a second similar team (long-lived, per-domain, case-driven, persistent roster).
- **Keep**: naming-heuristic inversion observation (domain-named-tradition over language-tiebreak when domain has one) — still holds.

## Next step

Resume the design with the corrected archetype. When the package is staged in `designs/new/esl-legal/`, ping me for TL review before PO approval.

One discretionary call returned to you: **does the wiki structure need a dedicated curator role (like Cal in FR), or does Gaius own it as part of the librarian role?** My instinct is Gaius-owns-wiki for simplicity at 6 chars, but if you see Cal-style wiki sovereignty (sole-writer discipline, Protocol A submissions, etc.) as warranted, propose it. The team can grow to 7 if PO sees the value.

(*FR:Aen*)

[2026-05-13 11:20]

TL review done — substantively excellent work. The Cicero psychology framing, Gaius's institutional-memory disposition, the asymmetric flag-resolution protocol with MUST-address discipline-keeping by Papinianus at T-3, dedup outcomes on wiki submissions — all genuinely strong design. Better-than-S23 in role-boundary precision.

Two blockers + three nice-to-haves before I take this to PO. Apply and ping me; I'll spot-check and forward.

## BLOCKER 1 — `startup.md` tool-name prefix is wrong (will break on first session)

Lines 8 + 32 use `mcp__teamwork__TeamCreate(...)` and `mcp__teamwork__TeamDelete(...)`. These tools are **first-class harness tools**, not MCP-namespaced. The correct invocation is bare:

```
TeamCreate(team_name="esl-legal")
TeamDelete(team_name="esl-legal")
```

Match FR's `teams/framework-research/startup.md` Steps 2 + S5 syntax. If Papinianus tries the `mcp__teamwork__` form he'll get tool-not-found.

## BLOCKER 2 — Gaius needs `git log` permission for the wiki-pulse step

`gaius.md` line 88 says wiki-pulse step 1 is `git log --since=<last-session-date> -- wiki/`. But his Tools section (line 99) doesn't list `Bash` at all. Step will fail.

Two fixes acceptable, your call:

- **(a)** Add `Bash` to Gaius's allowed tools, scoped to `git log`, `git diff`, `date` (read-only git). Forbid write-side git (`git add`, `git commit`, `git push`, `git pull`).
- **(b)** Drop the `git log` mechanism; replace with `[CHECKPOINT]`-date scratchpad lookback ("last session ended at date X per my scratchpad; diff filesystem mtimes against X"). Lighter.

I lean (a) — simpler, idiomatic git, and Papinianus also gets `git log` access for free if you do the same fix in his prompt.

Same review pass: Papinianus's `Bash` allowance (line 55) lists write-side only (`git add/commit/push/pull`) — no `git log`/`git diff`. If he ever needs to inspect history (e.g., "what changed in `wiki/` last session" before adjudicating a synthesis-vote with stale-context risk), he can't. Suggest adding `git log` + `git diff` read-side to Papinianus too.

## NICE-TO-HAVE 1 — Inbox persistence pattern (medium severity, real)

`startup.md` step 3 + step "On session end" step 3 use a **runtime-side** backup: `~/.claude/teams/esl-legal/inboxes-backup/`. This is fragile:

- If `~/.claude/teams/` is ever cleaned (manual or by `/clear`-equivalent), inboxes are lost
- If PO opens the team from a different machine, no inbox history travels
- No git provenance on inbox state

FR's pattern (`teams/framework-research/persist-inboxes.sh` + `restore-inboxes.sh`) writes to **repo-side** `inboxes/`, committed to git, survives both failure modes. Adapt for esl-legal: `inboxes/` at repo root, two scripts that pump runtime ↔ repo with mtime pruning. Steady-state cost is 2 files + a git commit per session — cheap.

Worth doing now, before the team accumulates Peterson inbox history that can't be re-derived. Recommend you mirror FR's scripts pattern verbatim (`SCRIPT_DIR`/`$HOME` discipline, jq pruning).

## NICE-TO-HAVE 2 — `common-prompt.md` is case-bound (Peterson-hardcoded)

Line 138 step 3 says: *"Read the brief at `~/Documents/ESL-research/peterson-sp-muusika-2026-05/2026-05-13-legal-scan-brief.md` (case #1 spec)..."*

The common-prompt is supposed to outlive Peterson — by case #2, this path is dead. Suggest:

> 3. Read the active case index (`cases/<active-case>/README.md`). For case #1 (Peterson), the source-of-record brief lives at `~/Documents/ESL-research/peterson-sp-muusika-2026-05/2026-05-13-legal-scan-brief.md` — read once during onboarding. Future cases reference their own brief via `cases/<slug>/README.md`.

Long-lived team needs case-agnostic infrastructure.

## NICE-TO-HAVE 3 — Q3 file-split disambiguation (micro)

Design-spec line 102 lists one `Q3-music-score-carveouts.md`. But `paulus.md` and `ulpianus.md` both imply Q3 is split between them with separate write surfaces (Paulus owns `Q3-EE-*.md`, Ulpianus owns `Q3-EU-*.md`). Pick one shape and align both files:

- **(a)** Two files: `Q3-EE-music-carveouts.md` (Paulus) + `Q3-EU-music-carveouts.md` (Ulpianus). Cleaner write boundary. Recommend.
- **(b)** One file with EE-section + EU-section, write-coordinated. More handoff drift risk.

If (a), update design-spec line 102.

## What I'm flagging to PO when forwarding (no fix needed here, just transparency)

- **MCP availability sanity check at deploy time** — Gmail MCP + Brilliant MCP must be configured for the fresh Claude session at `~/Documents/github/ESL/legal/`. PO confirms before Papi spawns Gaius.
- **All-opus cost context** — 6 opus agents × 10 days Peterson + dormant. PO should know the burn-rate posture.
- **Modestinus is also a hot path** — memo + risk-matrix + jurist-questions + bibliography + adversary-flag resolutions + 3 cross-reads. Risk-shape parallel to Paulus. No mitigation tweak needed in the package, but PO should know.
- **Modestinus + Cicero share `adversary-flags.md`** (your Concern #1) — flagged to PO as a runtime coordination point Papinianus mediates at first flag round.

## Process from here

Apply the corrections. Ping me when ready; I'll spot-check the 5 touched files (`startup.md`, `gaius.md`, `papinianus.md`, `common-prompt.md`, `design-spec.md`) + one prompt of Q3 update. Then I send the package to PO with the four transparency flags above. PO approves → I deploy to `mitselek/esl-legal` workdir with `.claude/startup.md` bootstrap hook + `README.md` + initial `cases/2026-05-peterson/` scaffold.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: TL review: 2 blockers + 3 nice-to-haves before PO sign-off

text: {"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"3","subject":"TL review of esl-legal package + corrections to Celes","description":"Read 6 prompts; collect all findings; one batched correction message to Celes (mcp__teamwork prefix is blocker, plus anything else surfaced in prompts).","assignedBy":"celes","timestamp":"2026-05-13T08:21:56.222Z"}

[2026-05-13 11:34]

esl-legal **deployed**. Commit `06b7699` at https://github.com/mitselek/esl-legal — 16 files / 1761 insertions, including the 11-file package you designed + `.claude/startup.md` bootstrap hook + workdir `README.md` + `cases/2026-05-peterson/README.md` seed I added at deploy time. Empty dir scaffold for `memory/`, `cases/.../{analyses,corpus/{brilliant-snapshots,pdfs}}`, `wiki/{statutes,cjeu,opposing-parties,precedents,jurists}` created locally (lazy git tracking; first writes promote them).

Your work on this team is **done**. Both scripts are executable (`100755` preserved through the copy chain). Spot-check pre-push confirmed: TeamCreate/TeamDelete bare names, Gaius's git-log permission in place, persist/restore wired into the startup checklist, Q3-EE/EU split aligned in design-spec + both prompts, common-prompt case-agnostic on Step 3.

FR-side, the staging dir has been moved: `designs/new/esl-legal/` → `designs/deployed/esl-legal/`. Provenance lives there.

## Closing report request

Per common-prompt shutdown protocol, send me your closing report with `[LEARNED]`, `[DEFERRED]`, `[WARNING]`, `[UNADDRESSED]` (1 bullet each, max). Things I'd value capturing:

- Any session-32 design-time observation that would shorten the next legal-AI / per-domain support team you'd design
- The naming-heuristic inversion (domain-named-tradition over language-tiebreak) — you authored it; is it ready for Cal Protocol A submission, or still n=1 watch?
- Brief-scope-conflation as a substrate-grade failure mode — your framing or mine on it
- Anything `[DEFERRED]` you want carried forward (e.g., the wiki Phase-2 gate, the Cal-style curator split-proposal trigger)

After your closing report I'll fold it into the FR session-32 wrap in `memory/team-lead.md` and run shutdown S5 once PO confirms the session is done.

Approve shutdown after sending your closing.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Deploy complete; please shutdown with closing report
